degraded: Gemini's external CODE review leg degenerated again: 612 characters, truncated mid-sentence, no coherent findings. This is the FOURTH consecutive sub-iterate (S2, S3, S4, S5), so it is a standing provider defect rather than a one-off. Recorded rather than re-rolled, and NOT counted as a passing review -- the external code review had ONE usable leg (GPT, 3 findings: two accepted-and-fixed, one false positive caused by my own exclusion of the 156 KB generated golden.json from the diff I sent, verified empirically). The PLAN review had BOTH legs substantive (Gemini 4 findings, GPT 11) and all 13 are dispositioned in ADR-108.

RESOLVED during the code-review round, recorded because the earlier numbers in this run were reported without it. plugins/shipwright-grade did not COLLECT (ModuleNotFoundError: defusedxml, 10 collection errors) and .shipwright/compliance/sbom.md regressed to 'Licenses resolved 3/11' with 8 packages 'unknown'. ONE root cause: the per-plugin .venv directories did not exist in this worktree, and the SBOM license resolver reads each manifest's own .venv dist-info METADATA (NOT ambient sys.path) by design. Fixed by syncing all 14 plugin venvs, not by declaring it: sbom.md is back to 11/11 resolved, 0 unknown, 3 unique licenses -- identical to the pre-change state -- and shipwright-grade now runs 385 passed / 1 skipped. The unit count above INCLUDES it (8159 -> 8558; the grade suite contributes 385 of that). The SBOM regression was the serious half: it is a shipped compliance artifact and it was reporting 8 licenses as unverified.

The outbox sweep at worktree setup reported protected_status_unplaceable for trg-b1dda91d and trg-16d374e1: status lines whose appends are in main's tracked log but not reachable from this branch. Pre-existing and unrelated to S5 (same condition S4 recorded); no triage append of mine was dropped. Left for the main-tree reconcile rather than worked around.
The Removed Requirements table shape was deliberately NOT converged. It carries a different job (Removed by, status), no AC asked for it, and changing it would have been scope creep. It still parses.

PROCESS NOTE, recorded because it nearly cost a review: this iterate amends into ONE commit and force-pushes, so a SHA reported in a status update ceases to exist at the next amend. I reported 'counts identical, doc-only' scoped to 9645933a..feb5afa0, which was true, but the range nobody had reviewed was bb1bf6cf..feb5afa0 -- the entire fix round produced in response to GPT and Codex, since those legs reviewed the commit BEFORE those fixes and generated them. The coordinator caught it and gated one more review on exactly that range, which found 9 issues including a blocking one. When amending, state the range that has NOT been reviewed, not the range since the last report.

Eight adopt scaffolders carry the same unguarded shared-module loader fixed in three places here (spec is None / spec.loader is None cannot fire for a missing path, so plugins-without-shared fails as FileNotFoundError instead of a named ImportError): automerge_setup_scaffolder, baseline_generator, ci_workflow_scaffolder, claude_review_workflow_scaffolder, codeql_workflow_scaffolder, gitattributes_scaffolder, gitleaks_config_scaffolder, security_workflow_scaffolder. ALL EIGHT ARE UNTOUCHED BY THIS PR -- merging makes nothing worse. Not folded in: eight unreviewed edits in a round scoped as 'these three, then ship' is the pattern that cost this campaign five review rounds. Found by sweeping for the pattern rather than fixing reported instances, which is how the count went from three to eleven.

surface_verification.note: The behavioural surface is the pair of PRODUCERS and the reader that consumes them. The suite drives the producer->file->consumer round trip for both sides of the (inferred) marker, asserts the post-migration provenance census on the real catalog (zero explicit), and includes a counter-probe proving the census guard can actually fail. The runner uses the main-tree interpreter by absolute path because the worktree venv has no pytest and the F0.5 runner executes without a shell.

behaviors.behavior: both producers and the live catalog carry one byte-identical header
the greenfield template AND its worked example both carry it
the adopt generator emits it (rendered through the real producer)
no producer still emits the retired Source column
the canonical header is readable by the reader that consumes it
every live Layers cell carries the literal (inferred) marker
ZERO explicit provenance across all 15 live requirements
counter-probe: stripping the marker really DOES flip rows to explicit (so the census guard is not vacuous)
required_layers unchanged per requirement (per-ID, not aggregate)
render_layers round-trips to the provenance it promises, both sides of the marker
the (inferred) marker is separated by a space -- a glued marker yields ZERO layers
only the parenthesised word counts: (auto)/(guess)/bare do NOT match
the marker matches case-insensitively and with inner space
a bare cell (author declaration) stays explicit -- the hard-gated form
every vocabulary value is known and never blocks
a value outside the vocabulary is hard
other carries its reason whatever separator was used
a bare other is advisory, not malformed
a word merely starting with 'other' is not other
a known value with a qualifier is hard but says why
I5 fails a malformed Basis and names the requirement
I5: other is advisory and never fails
I5: a blank cell under a DECLARED column fails (external review C1)
I5: a spec with no Basis column is skipped, not scored
I5 failing does not redden the advisory prose checks
Group I state: no spec on disk
Group I state: spec present but nothing FR-shaped
Group I state: FR ids present but no governing header
Group I state: header recognised but no canonical id (the S4-amended state)
Group I state precedence: a recognised header outranks a missing one
every state still SKIPs but names which
scan_fr_rows keeps its pre-S5 signature
Area is rendered from the group digit
a misfiled requirement renders its OWN group, not the folder's (D7)
a non-canonical id falls back to the split label
Basis: source_file -> code, url-only -> observed, neither -> assumed
Basis: source_file wins when both are present
a PLACEHOLDER source_file is not evidence (regression: the em-dash default)
a pipe in a description does not create a column (round-tripped)
no detected features still yields a well-formed table
layers follow the existing surface inference (shape changed, inference did not)
the golden corpus carries the converged shape as fixture 08
FV-2 stays frozen for Group I, but the skip now names its state
a row too narrow for its header is NOT reported as an id problem (fifth state; the branch reads `reason`, not `header_seen` alone)
a non-canonical id with no governing header is a HEADER problem
a glued (inferred) marker records the layer it swallowed (marker_glued), while provenance correctly stays advisory
a glued marker swallows the layer it touches — pinned as a FACT so the reason render_layers is mandatory cannot decay into style
a glued (inferred) marker is VISIBLE but does NOT fail the audit -- the fix for invisibility must not become a blocking path its own docs deny
an unrecognised invalid_layers reason FAILS CLOSED (the advisory set is the closed one, not the hard set)
a hard reason still decides the verdict when mixed with an advisory one, and both stay in evidence
an all-retired spec is not reported as having no FR-shaped rows
one live row beside retired ones is the normal state
a broken table outranks an all-retired one (a parse failure is a repair, a retirement is not)
the declined-id list quotes ONLY the ids of the reason that decided the state
a duplicate governed column resolves to the FIRST, so a second Layers column cannot flip a marked cell to explicit
Layer/Layers synonyms resolve by COLUMN position, not by synonym-tuple order
a marked cell whose tokens are not layer names is recorded (unknown_layer_token), not silently left with no coverage obligation
every live requirement carries its DECIDED Basis, per FR -- the durable record the throwaway migration script was not
no persisted Area cell disagrees with the value derived from its own id
the EVIDENCE line agrees with the verdict: an unrecognised reason renders [HARD, unknown], never `advisory` on a line that failed
the catch-all no_governing_header state still names the rows it declined (narrowing attribution must not cost attribution)
the mixed-reason wording drops the universal claim entirely, not just one clause of it
the catch-all state never claims rows it did not list -- its id list is complete, so the 'others' suffix must not render
CHARACTERIZED BLIND SPOT: a typo'd layer beside a valid one (`unit, e2ee`) is deliberately NOT reported -- the cost of the guard that removes the prose-connective false positive
